description_long: This church features a grand, elongated rectangular nave with a steeply pitched red-tiled roof and a prominent rose window above the main entrance. The facade is adorned with two tall, pointed towers capped with spires and crosses, flanking the central entrance with three arched doorways. The side walls are lined with evenly spaced rectangular windows and decorative buttresses, while the rear of the building showcases a polygonal apse with smaller annexes. The structure is primarily constructed from reddish-orange brick or stone, accented with lighter stone trim around windows, doors, and architectural details. The overall design is symmetrical and highly detailed, with Gothic Revival influences evident in the pointed arches, spires, and ornate window tracery. The church is imposing in scale, suggesting a significant presence within an urban neighborhood or city center.
description: A large, ornate church with red-tiled roofs, twin spired towers, and a detailed facade featuring a rose window and arched entrances. The structure is built from reddish brick with lighter stone accents and displays Gothic Revival architectural elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long nave, rose window, and detailed brickwork with the twin towers visible at the far end.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the two tall spired towers, three arched entrances, rose window, and symmetrical facade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with the nave, rose window, and towers visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the polygonal apse, annexes, and the backs of the twin towers with their spires and crosses.
